description_long: This is a single-story suburban bungalow with a broad, low-pitched gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade features a three-car garage with white doors, a small covered entryway with a white door, and a driveway with two parked cars. The exterior walls are clad in light blue horizontal siding, with a section of natural wood paneling near the main entrance. Several large, leafy trees are positioned around the house, providing shade and enhancing the residential feel. Windows are arranged in small groups or pairs, with some on the sides and rear, and a modest covered patio area is visible at the back. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of mid- to late-20th-century American suburban homes.
description: A single-story, light blue suburban bungalow with a gray gable roof, three-car garage, and surrounding trees. Features include a covered entry, wood paneling, and grouped windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three-car garage, driveway with two cars, main entrance with covered porch, and two large trees flanking the house.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wood-paneled entry section, a small covered porch, and a tree partially obscuring the garage area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a covered patio area, sliding glass doors, and a more extensive view of the backyard and grouped windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain blue siding, two small windows, and the arrangement of trees along the side of the house.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped side and rear windows
